metadata.title: The Wave Function
metadata.notes: Film four — the one the matter-waves outro promised. REMOTION PLANE ACCEPTANCE TEST: B05 is a true DOCUMENT·annotate beat (Born's footnote) and the tangent runs word-keyed spotlights; both degrade gracefully until the plane lands. Kennard deliberately excluded (ch 9's film). Tangent mid-film, after the footnote motivates the rule.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: In 1926, in Göttingen, Max Born wrote a paper about particles colliding. Correcting the proofs, he added a footnote. It became the most consequential edit in physics.
beats.tts_normalized_text: In nineteen twenty-six, in Göttingen, Max Born wrote a paper about particles colliding. Correcting the proofs, he added a footnote. It became the most consequential edit in physics.

shot.note: AI-generated portrayal of Born (PORTRAIT preset) — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] Max Born ~1926, Göttingen study, mid-forties, wire glasses; set shot.focus on his face

beats.narration_text: Last film ended with a question: if every particle is a wave, what exactly is waving? The answer has a name. The wave function.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Last film ended with a question: if every particle is a wave, what exactly is waving? The answer has a name. The wave function.

beats.narration_text: That same year, Erwin Schrödinger produced an equation for a new object: psi. It said exactly how psi moves. It never said what psi was.
beats.tts_normalized_text: That same year, Erwin Schrödinger produced an equation for a new object: sigh. It said exactly how sigh moves. It never said what sigh was.

shot.note: AI-generated portrayal of Schrödinger (PORTRAIT preset) — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] Erwin Schrödinger ~1926, round glasses, blackboard behind; set shot.focus on his face

beats.narration_text: Here it is, from our own simulation: a blue hump drifting and spreading, an orange ripple running inside it. Beautiful mathematics. But what do the curves mean?
beats.tts_normalized_text: Here it is, from our own simulation: a blue hump drifting and spreading, an orange ripple running inside it. Beautiful mathematics. But what do the curves mean?

viz.data: Gaussian packet, k0 > 0: |ψ|² blue filled, Re ψ orange line, Im ψ gray dashed — drifting right and spreading; big '?' label chip lands on the spoken 'mean'
viz.note: Mirrors the ch 0 simulation palette exactly (blue filled / orange / gray dashed) — series continuity

beats.narration_text: Born's draft said the probability of finding the particle is proportional to psi. The footnote fixed it: not psi — psi squared. One symbol, added in proof.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Born's draft said the probability of finding the particle is proportional to sigh. The footnote fixed it: not sigh — sigh squared. One symbol, added in proof.
beats.new_visual_element: [SCAN] Born, Z. Phys. 37, 863 (1926) — the page carrying the 'Anmerkung bei der Korrektur' footnote. REMOTION PLANE: highlight sweep on the ψ claim keyed to 'psi', ring the squared correction keyed to 'squared', push into the footnote. Degrades to hold+slow-zoom until the plane lands. Regions in annotations.json are needs_review until the scan is in hand.

beats.narration_text: Born's rule is one line: the probability of finding the particle in a region is the integral of psi squared over that region. Odds, not certainty.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Born's rule is one line: the probability of finding the particle in a region is the integral of sigh squared over that region. Odds, not certainty.

tangent.lhs: The odds of finding it between a and b.
tangent.rhs: Add up psi squared across that stretch.
tangent.claim: Psi never says where the particle IS — only the odds of where you'll FIND it.

glossary.mean: the wave function — what Schrödinger's equation moves
probability per nanometer — not a probability
sum the density over your region — THIS makes a probability

example.rhs_val: peak density |ψ(0)|² = 1/a = 2 nm⁻¹ — exceeds 1, and that's legal
example.verdict: a density may exceed 1 — a probability never does
example.cost: Read the density as a probability and you've made a units error — like reading a city's population off a density map at one address.

beats.narration_text: Psi is the wave function. Psi squared is a density — probability per nanometer, like population per square mile. The integral adds it up over your region.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Sigh is the wave function. Sigh squared is a density — probability per nanometer, like population per square mile. The integral adds it up over your region.

beats.narration_text: Take psi as a falling exponential. Within one width of center: eighty-six percent. At the peak the density reads two per nanometer — bigger than one, perfectly legal.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Take sigh as a falling exponential. Within one width of center: eighty-six percent. At the peak the density reads two per nanometer — bigger than one, perfectly legal.

beats.narration_text: Here's the strange part: psi is complex. Two packets, identical blue humps. One moves left, one moves right. The hump can't tell you which. The phase can.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Here's the strange part: sigh is complex. Two packets, identical blue humps. One moves left, one moves right. The hump can't tell you which. The phase can.

viz.data: Side-by-side packets, k0 = +5 and −5 nm⁻¹: identical blue |ψ|² humps drifting opposite ways; phase ripples (orange) tilt opposite; crimson/navy direction arrows land on 'left'/'right'
viz.note: Chapter: |ψ|² = A²e^(−x²/a²) — k0 absent from the density; direction is pure phase

beats.narration_text: That's not decoration. Schrödinger's equation has an i built in — erase the imaginary part and take one time-step: it grows right back. Motion lives in the phase.
beats.tts_normalized_text: That's not decoration. Schrödinger's equation has an i built in — erase the imaginary part and take one time-step: it grows right back. Motion lives in the phase.

viz.data: iℏ ∂ψ/∂t = Ĥψ card, the i ringed (hand-drawn, terracotta); below: Im ψ (gray dashed) flattened to zero, one time-step ticks, it springs back
viz.note: NOT a tangent — the equation is shown, not taught; ch 4's film owns it. Real ψ ⇒ LHS imaginary, RHS real ⇒ no dynamics (chapter).

beats.narration_text: And when the blue hump spreads, the particle is not smearing out. Detect it: one dot. Repeat the experiment thousands of times — the dots build psi squared.
beats.tts_normalized_text: And when the blue hump spreads, the particle is not smearing out. Detect it: one dot. Repeat the experiment thousands of times — the dots build sigh squared.

viz.data: Spreading |ψ|² envelope; single dots land one at a time, histogram bars grow beneath until they trace the curve — deliberate echo of film three's Tonomura buildup
viz.note: Sampling weighted by |ψ|² — same technique as B08_TheBuildup in vox-matter-waves

beats.narration_text: The wave function is not the particle. It is the rule for the odds. Every histogram ever measured agrees with the footnote.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The wave function is not the particle. It is the rule for the odds. Every histogram ever measured agrees with the footnote.

shot.note: AI-generated plate — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO+ANNOTATION·ai] detector plate macro, faint dot accumulation; chips 'NOT the particle / THE RULE for the odds' land at assembly (Remotion plane; degrades to clean plate)

beats.narration_text: Who was Max Born? When Einstein wrote that God does not play dice, the letter was addressed to Born. His Nobel came twenty-eight years after the footnote.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Who was Max Born? When Einstein wrote that God does not play dice, the letter was addressed to Born. His Nobel came twenty-eight years after the footnote.

shot.note: AI-generated portrayal of Born (PORTRAIT preset, direct gaze) — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] Max Born kicker portrait, direct gaze; kicker typography (name · 1882–1970 · the one line) on the Remotion plane; degrades to serif label at compile

beats.narration_text: Next: the equation that moves the wave function — where it came from, and why it works.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Next: the equation that moves the wave function — where it came from, and why it works.
